Checklist Item: Semantic-to-div ratio above threshold; pages with >70% generic containers (div/span) flagged
Comments: blocks/: 55 div/span vs 39 semantic creates (~59% generic); scripts/: 11 div/span vs 23 semantic. Combined ~51% generic, below 70% threshold.
Evidence: blocks/, scripts

Checklist Item: Correct heading hierarchy maintained
Comments: Blocks use proper h2/h3/h4 hierarchy. custom-title, carousel, header, teaser all query for existing headings and preserve them rather than re-creating levels. No h1 created programmatically inside blocks.
Evidence: blocks/custom-title/custom-title.js:11, blocks/brightcove-video/brightcove-video.js:943, blocks/pipeline-utility-nav/pipeline-utility-nav.js:179

Checklist Item: Single h1 per page where applicable
Comments: No block programmatically creates an h1. H1 comes from authored content only. Blocks query heading generically (h1-h6) without injecting additional h1 elements.
Evidence: blocks/ (no createElement('h1') found)

Checklist Item: DOM source order matches visual tab order; no positive tabindex values; CSS order/flex-direction does not contradict DOM sequence
Comments: story-card.css uses flex-direction: column-reverse (lines 63, 680) and row-reverse (line 945) which reverses visual order relative to DOM. No positive tabindex values found; tabindex usage is correctly -1/0 only.
Evidence: blocks/story-card/story-card.css:63, blocks/story-card/story-card.css:680, blocks/story-card/story-card.css:945

Checklist Item: Page content hierarchy intact: headings and body text present in raw HTML source; critical content not injected solely via JavaScript; no content exclusively hidden via inline style or JS-only class toggling
Comments: Content headings/text come from authored HTML, not JS injection. JS-only style.display='none' is limited to UI controls (pagination, error msgs, loaders) not main content. breadcrumb hides block conditionally (expected behaviour for empty breadcrumb). eds-form visibility:hidden is a temporary state during load.
Evidence: blocks/breadcrumb/breadcrumb.js:256, blocks/eds-form/eds-form.js:779

Checklist Item: Avoid excessive DOM depth
Comments: 151 total appendChild calls across all blocks. Largest blocks (linklist, story-card, pipeline-utility-nav) each have ~27-42 DOM mutations — typical for complex components. No evidence of pathological nesting patterns.
Evidence: blocks/ (151 total appendChild calls)

Checklist Item: Radio/checkbox groups wrapped in fieldset with legend; forms with 3+ related inputs have grouping
Comments: Both form blocks (form/form-fields.js and eds-form/eds-form.js) create fieldset with legend for radio/checkbox groups. eds-form.js:141 explicitly wraps radio/checkbox options in a fieldset with a legend.
Evidence: blocks/form/form-fields.js:163-169, blocks/eds-form/eds-form.js:141-158

Checklist Item: All inputs have associated labels
Comments: form/form-fields.js creates labels with for= attribute linked to input IDs and uses aria-labelledby. eds-form/eds-form.js uses buildLabel() which sets for= on all inputs. Radio/checkbox options each get individual labels wrapping the input.
Evidence: blocks/form/form-fields.js:23-30, blocks/eds-form/eds-form.js:48-53

Checklist Item: Placeholder not used as label
Comments: form/form-fields.js sets placeholder as supplementary (fd.Placeholder) alongside a proper label (fd.Label || fd.Name). Labels are always created separately with for= attribute. No instance of placeholder replacing label found.
Evidence: blocks/form/form-fields.js:23-37

Checklist Item: Error messages programmatically associated with inputs via aria-describedby or aria-errormessage; error containers use role='alert' or aria-live='assertive'; error text is non-empty and >10 characters
Comments: Error messages (.field-error) are not linked to inputs via aria-describedby or aria-errormessage. eds-form.js:687 only sets aria-describedby for help text, not errors. Evidence: blocks/eds-form/eds-form.js:679-687, blocks/form/form.js (no aria-describedby for errors)

Checklist Item: Avoid inline styles
Comments: Multiple inline style usages found: brightcove-video sets backgroundColor dynamically (line 347); press-releases uses style.transform/transition/userSelect for slider animation; find-an-injector sets borderColor inline (line 490). Checklist Item: Accessible names for interactive elements
Comments: Most interactive elements have accessible names (aria-label, textContent, or innerHTML with text). Issues: (1) tag-utility-nav clearBtn has no aria-label or text content (line 99-117); (2) cards/cards.js play buttons, accordion expand buttons all have aria-labels. clearBtn is icon-only with no accessible name.

Checklist Item: Images with role='presentation' or aria-hidden='true' have empty alt; standalone without adjacent text and without alt flagged; hero/banner images verified to have non-empty alt >10 chars; in decorative containers verified to have empty alt or role='presentation'
Comments: Blocks consistently handle alt text: custom-image supports imageIsDecorative flag (empty alt), carousel/hero-container fall back to title/textContent when alt missing, story-card/quote/brightcove-video all set meaningful alt text. fact-card correctly sets empty alt for decorative images.

Checklist Item: Alt text is not a filename or path; not generic (image/photo/icon/picture/graphic/banner/logo without qualifier); between 5-125 characters; does not duplicate adjacent text content; does not begin with 'image of'/'picture of'/'photo of'; alt on linked images describes destination
Comments: No hardcoded generic alt values (image/photo/icon/banner) found in JS. Alt text is sourced from authored content (cfg.posterAlt, data.ctaAltText, config.placeholderAlt, video.name, link title/textContent). No filename-as-alt patterns detected. Quality depends on author input, but the code infrastructure is correct.
Evidence: blocks/ (all alt values sourced from authored data, not hardcoded generic strings)

Checklist Item: Tables use thead and tbody

Checklist Item: Lists use ul or ol correctly
Comments: Blocks use ul/ol/li correctly: cards uses ul+li for card grid, carousel uses ul for slides and ol for pagination indicators, search uses ul for results, linklist/story-cards use ul+li. li elements are always children of ul/ol.

Comments: In AEM EDS, the page title is derived from the authored document title (Google Docs/SharePoint) and injected by the platform into <title> automatically. head.html contains no static title tag, which is correct EDS pattern. No default/generic titles are hardcoded in the codebase.

Checklist Item: Meta viewport configured correctly
Comments: head.html contains: <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1"/> — correct standard viewport configuration.

Checklist Item: No duplicate IDs
Comments: brightcove-video.js assigns static IDs 'primaryVideoTitle' and 'primaryVideoDesc' (lines 944, 948). If multiple brightcove-video blocks exist on a page, duplicate IDs will result. carousel uses carouselId-based IDs (safe). Form blocks use generateId/generateFieldId functions (safe). header uses metadata-driven ID (potentially safe but single instance assumed).
Evidence: blocks/brightcove-video/brightcove-video.js:944-948

Checklist Item: No invalid HTML nesting
Comments: header.js:658 appends an <a> element inside a <button> (interactive content inside interactive content — invalid HTML per spec). This is a language-links special case but still invalid nesting. embed.js:186 sets innerHTML with button inside div (valid). No other major nesting violations found.
Evidence: blocks/header/header.js:658 (a nested inside button)

Checklist Item: HTML validates without errors
Comments: Known issues: (1) header.js:658 nests <a> inside <button> (invalid HTML); (2) 404.html uses deprecated type="text/javascript" attribute on script tag (unnecessary but minor). HTML structure is generally valid across blocks. No validator was run against live pages — static code analysis only.
Evidence: blocks/header/header.js:658, 404.html:6

Checklist Item: ARIA roles do not duplicate native semantics
Comments: Multiple issues: (1) footer.js:34 sets role='button' on an <a> — native anchor role is 'link', role='button' override is redundant since href is also removed; should use a <button> instead. (2) story-card.js:892, teaser.js:42/72, fact-card.js:79/187, pipeline-utility-nav.js:362 set role='heading' on non-heading elements without aria-level — missing required aria-level attribute. (3) linklist.js:1252 sets role='button' on <a> — same pattern as footer.
Evidence: blocks/footer/footer.js:34, blocks/story-card/story-card.js:892, blocks/linklist/linklist.js:1252

Checklist Item: Avoid misuse of ARIA roles
Comments: All role='heading' usages are accompanied by aria-level (story-card:893, teaser:43/73, brightcove-podcast-player:277, fact-card:80/188, pipeline-utility-nav:363). role='switch' on toggle input (eds-form) is valid. role='tablist'/'tab'/'tabpanel' in tabs.js is correct. role='region' with aria-label is valid. No misused roles found.
Evidence: blocks/story-card/story-card.js:892-893, blocks/teaser/teaser.js:42-43

Checklist Item: ARIA used only when necessary
Comments: ARIA usage appears purposeful: aria-expanded for dropdowns/nav, aria-controls for relationships, aria-labelledby for regions, aria-roledescription for carousel. eds-form uses aria-checked for role='switch' toggle which is required by the switch role spec. No redundant aria-label on elements already labeled by visible text detected.
Evidence: blocks/eds-form/eds-form.js:267-271, blocks/carousel/carousel.js:749

Checklist Item: Meta tags do not block zoom
Comments: No user-scalable=no, maximum-scale, or minimum-scale found in any HTML or JS file. head.html viewport only sets width=device-width, initial-scale=1.

Checklist Item: No broken links (run broken-link-checker or linkinator CLI against local build or source HTML)

Recommend running: npx linkinator https://[site-domain] --recurse after deploy.

Checklist Item: Consistent naming conventions
Comments: Block folder/file names consistently use kebab-case. CSS class names mostly follow kebab-case block-name prefixing. Checklist Item: Class names do not contain hex values or color names as identifiers; no purely positional names (left1/col2); BEM or project naming convention pattern enforced

Checklist Item: CSS selectors for same component co-located in same file; no duplicate selectors scattered across files; related rules grouped by component
Comments: Each block has its own CSS file co-located in the block folder. Checklist Item: Layout containers use display:flex or display:grid; no float:left/right for multi-column layouts; no display:table for non-tabular content; absolute positioning in <5% of layout containers
Comments: eds-form.css uses float:left (lines 57, 62, 133, 191, 245, 296) and float:right (line 312) for layout, and display:table (line 588) for non-tabular content — both are legacy patterns. Flex/grid are used extensively (232 instances). Absolute positioning: 81 instances vs 232 flex/grid, giving ~26% ratio which exceeds the 5% guideline for layout containers.
Evidence: blocks/eds-form/eds-form.css:57-312 (float layout), blocks/eds-form/eds-form.css:588 (display:table)

Checklist Item: min-width media queries outnumber max-width queries; base styles (outside media queries) target mobile viewport dimensions
Comments: 179 media queries across blocks, all using custom media tokens --bp-tablet (min-width: 744px) and --bp-desktop (min-width: 1024px) defined in tokens.css. Zero max-width queries found. Fully mobile-first approach with min-width breakpoints only.

Checklist Item: Standard breakpoints only
Comments: Non-standard breakpoints found: tabs.css, form.css, table.css use 600px and 900px (not in tokens.css); stock-ticker.css uses a range query (744px <= width <= 1279px); modal.css uses 900px; cards.css uses max-width direction (width < 744px). Checklist Item: Responsive images by default
Comments: createOptimizedPicture() from aem.js is used across blocks (cards, carousel, search, brightcove-podcast-player, story-cards) to generate responsive picture elements with srcset. EDS platform serves images through the media delivery pipeline with automatic format conversion and responsive sizing.
Evidence: blocks/cards/cards.js:196, blocks/carousel/carousel.js:503, blocks/search/search.js:94

Checklist Item: Avoid z-index escalation
Comments: z-index values are mostly 0-20 range. One outlier: eds-form.css:408 uses z-index:100 (likely for modal map overlay). No runaway escalation (999, 9999) detected. Values appear purposeful and scoped within their stacking contexts.
Evidence: blocks/eds-form/eds-form.css:408 (highest at 100, acceptable for form overlay)

Checklist Item: Avoid expensive box-shadows
Comments: Only 5 box-shadow usages found. All are simple single-layer shadows (0 0 blur or 0 offset blur) — no stacked multi-layer shadows or large spread values that would be expensive. No blur values > 20px detected.
Evidence: blocks/eds-form/eds-form.css:138, blocks/pipeline-utility-nav/pipeline-utility-nav.css:219

Checklist Item: Avoid heavy CSS filters
Comments: Only 2 filter usages: both are backdrop-filter:blur() with small values (var(--corp-spacing-2) and 5px). No heavy multi-function filters (brightness+contrast+blur combos), no filter:drop-shadow on large elements. Low performance impact expected.
Evidence: blocks/video/video.css:301, blocks/header/header.css:603

Checklist Item: No infinite CSS animations on non-loading elements; animation duration under 1s; total animation count per page within threshold
Comments: 2 infinite animations found: both are the 'spin' loader animation in eds-form.css (lines 424, 553) applied to .abbv-animation-loading and .loading classes — these are loading spinners shown only during async operations, which is acceptable. No infinite animations on content elements found. No animation durations over 1s detected outside the spin loader.
Evidence: blocks/eds-form/eds-form.css:418-424 (spin on .loading-overlay only)

Checklist Item: All CSS properties/values supported in target browsers (checked against caniuse compatibility data); no unprefixed experimental features without fallbacks
Comments: :has() pseudo-class used extensively in cards.css, story-card.css, embed.css. :has() requires Chrome 105+, Safari 15.4+, Firefox 121+ — Safari 15.4+ has ~95% global coverage but some enterprise users may be on older versions. No browserslist config found. @custom-media is used for breakpoints (PostCSS transform required). No fallbacks provided for :has() usage.
Evidence: blocks/cards/cards.css:21 (:has() without fallback), blocks/story-card/story-card.css:397, no .browserslistrc

Checklist Item: Avoid vendor prefixes unless required
Comments: Zero vendor-prefixed CSS properties found across all block and style CSS files. No -webkit-, -moz-, -ms-, or -o- prefixes used.

Checklist Item: No duplicated CSS rules
Comments: Duplicate CSS rule selectors found in brightcove-video.css (5 duplicates including .bcvideo-overlay-content, .bcvideo-primary-title), header.css (5 duplicates including .mega-menu-card variants), and eds-form.css (5 duplicates including .submit-wrapper, .eds-form form). These duplicates may be across media query blocks but indicate CSS maintenance issues.
Evidence: blocks/brightcove-video/brightcove-video.css, blocks/header/header.css, blocks/eds-form/eds-form.css (duplicate selectors found)

Checklist Item: No unused CSS selectors
Comments: Cannot run PurgeCSS without full page HTML content (requires live pages). Static analysis shows large CSS files (brightcove-video.css, header.css, eds-form.css) with many selectors that are difficult to verify without runtime. Duplicate selectors noted in row 45 suggest CSS was written incrementally without cleanup. No automated unused CSS check is configured in the project build pipeline.
Evidence: No purgecss config found, no stylelint unused-selector rules configured in project

Checklist Item: Consistent property ordering
Comments: stylelint config uses stylelint-config-standard (which enforces property order) but does NOT include stylelint-order plugin or order/properties-order rule. No property ordering enforcement is in place. stylelint-config-standard enforces many style rules but property ordering is not configured.
Evidence: .stylelintrc.json (no order rule configured)

Checklist Item: No blocking scripts in head
Comments: head.html scripts (aem.js, scripts.js) both use type="module" which is inherently deferred — no render blocking. 404.html has a plain type="text/javascript" inline script (line 6) but it only sets window globals; minimal blocking impact. No external blocking script tags in head.
Evidence: head.html:3-4 (type="module"), 404.html:6 (inline script, low impact)

Checklist Item: Use async or defer appropriately
Comments: All scripts in head.html use type="module" which provides implicit defer behaviour. Dynamically imported blocks are loaded on-demand via EDS loadBlock() pattern. No async/defer misuse detected.
Evidence: head.html:3-4

Checklist Item: Third-party scripts lazy loaded
Comments: Brightcove Player SDK is loaded on-demand via loadBrightcoveScript() (brightcove-video.js:230) — script is injected only when the block decorates. EDS loads all blocks lazily via loadBlock(). No third-party scripts found in head.html. No analytics/tag manager scripts hardcoded in the codebase.
Evidence: blocks/brightcove-video/brightcove-video.js:230-243

Checklist Item: No JS files loaded on pages where their exports are unused; code-split bundles match route usage
Comments: EDS architecture loads blocks on-demand via dynamic import in loadBlock(). scripts.js uses dynamic import() for modal (line 226) and delayed.js (line 497, 3s delayed). No monolithic bundle loading all blocks upfront. Block JS is only loaded when the block is present on the page.
Evidence: scripts/scripts.js:226 (dynamic import for modal), scripts/scripts.js:497 (delayed import)

Checklist Item: No marketing tags in head
Comments: No marketing tags (GTM, GA, Facebook Pixel, Hotjar, etc.) found in head.html or scripts.js. Tag management is expected to be handled via OneTrust/consent layer separately.
Evidence: head.html, scripts/scripts.js (no marketing tag references)

Checklist Item: User-facing strings in component render/template functions reference i18n keys or constants; hardcoded display strings >3 words flagged
Comments: 16 fetchPlaceholders usages found (good). However, multiple hardcoded user-facing strings >3 words exist: pipeline-utility-nav.js hardcodes 'Pipeline', 'Search Pipeline', 'SORT BY', 'Focus Area', 'FILTER', 'Clear All'; brightcove-video.js hardcodes 'Loading transcript…' and 'Transcript could not be loaded.'; tag-utility-nav.js hardcodes 'No results found' and 'Change your search criteria.'; header.js hardcodes 'Quick Links'. These should use placeholders for i18n support.
Evidence: blocks/pipeline-utility-nav/pipeline-utility-nav.js:181-327, blocks/tag-utility-nav/tag-utility-nav.js:135-141, blocks/brightcove-video/brightcove-video.js:525

Checklist Item: No binaries stored in repository
Comments: Repository contains only expected binary assets: favicon.ico, font files (woff2, ttf for brand fonts Roboto and F37Lineca, icon font). No images, PDFs, ZIPs, or other unexpected binaries in the repo. Font files in /fonts/ are standard and necessary for the EDS self-hosted font strategy.
Evidence: fonts/ directory (woff2/ttf font files only), favicon.ico

Checklist Item: Error messages follow consistent pattern (checked via regex); no raw string throws; error constants or error classes used
Comments: All errors use new Error() (no raw string throws — good). However, no consistent error message pattern across blocks: stock-ticker uses [StockTicker] prefix, brightcove-video uses plain strings, eds-form uses HTTP status codes, news-feed uses 'Request failed with status'. No shared error constants or error class hierarchy. Messages are inconsistent in format and level of detail.
Evidence: blocks/stock-ticker/stock-ticker.js:157 ([prefix] pattern), blocks/brightcove-video/brightcove-video.js:261 (no prefix), blocks/news-feed/news-feed.js:17

Checklist Item: Avoid tight DOM coupling
Comments: 43 global document.querySelector/getElementById calls found. Most blocks correctly scope to block element. However, eds-form/find-an-injector.js and maps.js make multiple global document.querySelector calls (lines 21, 36, 44, 53, 54, 55, 64, 203, 218) — coupling across component boundaries. These should use scoped block element instead of document-wide queries.
Evidence: blocks/eds-form/find-an-injector.js:21-218 (global document queries), blocks/eds-form/maps.js:45-203

Checklist Item: Avoid forced synchronous layouts
Comments: Layout reads (getBoundingClientRect, offsetWidth, scrollTop) are present in carousel.js, press-releases.js, linklist.js, modal.js. These are used in scroll/resize event handlers and one-time measurements during block setup, not interleaved with writes in tight loops. press-releases caches slideWidth (line 119). No clear read-write-read-write layout thrashing patterns detected in code structure.
Evidence: blocks/carousel/carousel.js:222-274, blocks/press-releases/press-releases.js:119 (cached)

Checklist Item: All setTimeout/setInterval calls have corresponding clear calls; timer count per file within threshold (max 3); no timers <100ms
Comments: find-an-injector.js has 3 setTimeout calls (lines 434, 466, 567) with no corresponding clearTimeout — potential timer leak. eds-form/maps.js setInterval cleared properly. carousel setInterval cleared properly. No timeouts <100ms found. find-an-injector.js has 3 timers (at threshold) but none are cleared. modal.js setTimeout has no clear call. editorial-feed and header debounce patterns correctly use clearTimeout before setTimeout.
Evidence: blocks/eds-form/find-an-injector.js:434-567 (3 uncleaned setTimeouts), blocks/modal/modal.js:85

Checklist Item: setInterval for DOM/state checks flagged (suggest MutationObserver/IntersectionObserver/EventListener); polling intervals <1s flagged
Comments: maps.js and recaptcha.js both use setInterval at 100ms (below 1s threshold) to poll for window.google.maps and window.grecaptcha readiness — both are DOM/global state checks that should use script.onload callback or a Promise-based pattern instead. Both clear the interval when resolved, but the polling approach and 100ms interval are flagged.
Evidence: blocks/eds-form/maps.js:12 (100ms poll for Google Maps), blocks/eds-form/recaptcha.js:9 (100ms poll for reCAPTCHA)

Checklist Item: Prefer native browser APIs
Comments: No jQuery, lodash, or utility library imports found. All imports are from local scripts/ modules (aem.js, scripts.js, placeholders.js). Blocks use native browser APIs: fetch, IntersectionObserver, MutationObserver, native DOM methods, ES6 modules. No external utility dependencies detected.
Evidence: blocks/scripts/* — no third-party utility libs)

Checklist Item: Modern ES6+ syntax used
Comments: Zero var declarations found — all code uses const/let. 763 arrow functions and async/await usages. ES6 modules (import/export) used throughout. 35 .then/.catch usages are acceptable (mixed with async/await for compatibility). Destructuring, template literals, spread operators are used consistently.
Evidence: blocks/ (0 var declarations, 763 ES6+ syntax usages)

Checklist Item: Avoid memory leaks
Comments: 123 addEventListener calls vs only 1 removeEventListener (video.js:269). 11 window/document-level event listeners added but never removed — these persist for the page lifetime. EDS blocks are loaded and may be re-decorated; without cleanup this causes listener accumulation. No AbortController or { once: true } pattern used to manage listener lifecycle except in isolated cases.
Evidence: 123 addEventListener vs 1 removeEventListener across blocks

Checklist Item: Event listeners cleaned up properly
Comments: Only 2 files (video.js, carousel.js) use removeEventListener or {once:true}. The vast majority of blocks add event listeners with no cleanup on destroy or re-decoration. No AbortController pattern exists. Window/document-level listeners (resize, scroll, click, keydown) in header, footer, carousel, and other blocks are never removed.
Evidence: removeEventListener found only in blocks/video/video.js and blocks/carousel/carousel.js

Checklist Item: Async logic uses async/await
Comments: 30 files use async/await but 9 files still mix in .then()/.catch() chains (27 occurrences across brightcove-video.js, video.js, press-releases.js, carousel.js, eds-form.js, maps.js, recaptcha.js, embed-form.js, brightcove-podcast-player.js). Mixed async patterns reduce readability and error-handling consistency.
Evidence: 27 .then()/.catch() chains across 9 block JS files alongside async/await usage

Checklist Item: Promises handled correctly
Comments: Multiple fetch() calls lack error handling — 25 fetch usages found, none chained directly with .catch(); brightcove-video.js has several .catch(()=>{}) that silently swallow errors. Some .then() chains have no rejection handler. Unhandled promise rejections can cause silent failures in content loading and form submission.
Evidence: 25 fetch() calls across blocks/ without paired .catch(); brightcove-video.js:533,675,835 use .catch(()=>{}) silently

Checklist Item: State changes occur through defined setters/reducers/actions; no direct state object property mutations outside state management layer
Comments: No formal state management layer exists. Blocks manage state via local variables and direct DOM mutation, which is standard for EDS vanilla JS. However, global state is mutated directly: window.providerToMarkerMap (maps.js:271), window.filterOptions (find-an-injector.js:758), and window.dataLayer (video.js:187). No setters, reducers, or actions defined — mutations happen freely at module scope.
Evidence: window.providerToMarkerMap= in blocks/eds-form/maps.js:271; window.filterOptions= in blocks/eds-form/find-an-injector.js:758

Checklist Item: No mutation of shared state
Comments: Shared/global state is directly mutated: window.providerToMarkerMap, window.filterOptions, window.dataLayer, and the GTM dataLayer array are mutated by multiple blocks without coordination. No immutability patterns or shared-state contracts in place.
Evidence: window.providerToMarkerMap= in maps.js:271; window.filterOptions= in find-an-injector.js:758; window.dataLayer mutations in video.js and brightcove-podcast-player.js

Checklist Item: No side effects in pure functions
Comments: Multiple helper functions produce side effects: find-an-injector.js utility functions (showLoader, hideLoader, showError) directly query and mutate global DOM nodes rather than operating on passed elements. window.providerToMarkerMap and window.filterOptions are mutated inside functions ostensibly serving other purposes. No separation between pure computation and DOM/state side effects.
Evidence: find-an-injector.js:21-218 — helper functions call document.querySelector() and mutate DOM directly; maps.js:271 mutates window.providerToMarkerMap inside a setup function

Checklist Item: Consistent return types
Comments: No TypeScript, so return types are unenforced. Functions return inconsistent types: null, false, undefined, empty string, and DOM elements are returned interchangeably across 143 occurrences of null/false/undefined/''. No JSDoc @returns annotations found to document intended return types.
Evidence: 143 mixed null/false/undefined/'' return statements across blocks/ JS files; no TypeScript or JSDoc @returns in use

Checklist Item: No global variables
Comments: No bare var declarations at file scope (all files are ES modules so top-level const/let are module-scoped). However, true globals are created via window.* assignments: window.providerToMarkerMap (maps.js:271) and window.filterOptions (find-an-injector.js:758). These pollute the global namespace and create cross-block coupling.
Evidence: window.providerToMarkerMap= in blocks/eds-form/maps.js:271; window.filterOptions= in blocks/eds-form/find-an-injector.js:758

Checklist Item: JS scoped to component or block
Comments: Most blocks correctly scope JS to the passed block element. However, 28 document.querySelector() calls reach outside block scope: find-an-injector.js has 20+ global DOM queries; linklist.js:1308 queries a dialog outside its block; modal.js:52 appends to document main; maps.js:64 queries .fap-toggleMap globally. This creates tight coupling between blocks.
Evidence: 28 document.querySelector() calls across blocks/ breaking block isolation; worst in blocks/eds-form/find-an-injector.js (20+ occurrences)

Checklist Item: Consistent coding style
Comments: ESLint configured with airbnb-base + xwalk/recommended enforcing consistent style. Unix linebreaks enforced, JS extensions required in imports, no-param-reassign allowing prop mutations. Some rules relaxed for eds-form, stock-ticker, and theme-tools via overrides. No console.log calls found in blocks. Style is generally consistent across the codebase.
Evidence: .eslintrc.js — airbnb-base + xwalk/recommended; 0 console.log calls in blocks

Checklist Item: Avoid deeply nested callbacks
Comments: Most blocks use async/await avoiding callback nesting, but find-an-injector.js contains setTimeout wrapping async callbacks (lines 434, 466, 567) adding unnecessary nesting. No true callback pyramid-of-doom, but the setTimeout-wrapping-async pattern is an antipattern that adds unnecessary indirection and makes error handling harder.
Evidence: blocks/eds-form/find-an-injector.js:434,466,567 — setTimeout wrapping async callbacks

Checklist Item: Call chain depth <=4 levels; wrapper functions that only delegate flagged; files with >80% re-exports flagged
Comments: EDS block files follow a flat module pattern — each block exports a single decorate() function with helpers defined in the same file. No re-export barrel files found. Call chains are generally shallow (decorate → helper functions). story-card.js exports 3 named functions for cross-block use, which is appropriate. No excessive delegation wrappers detected.
Evidence: All block JS files export single decorate() function; no re-export index files; story-card.js:1078-1103 exports 3 named functions for legitimate cross-block use

Checklist Item: All external inputs are validated, sanitized, and securely handled to prevent errors, misuse, and security vulnerabilities
Comments: No DOMPurify or HTML sanitization library is used. 47 innerHTML assignments found; most use static strings or AEM-authored content (trusted), but brightcove-video.js:366,919 set innerHTML from cfg.overlayDescription which is read from block DOM innerHTML — an unsanitized passthrough. embed.js:85 sets innerHTML from a URL-derived embed string. No input validation layer for URL parameters or API responses before they reach the DOM.
Evidence: brightcove-video.js:366,919 — desc.innerHTML = cfg.overlayDescription (read from block DOM unsanitized); embed.js:85 — block.innerHTML from URL; no DOMPurify or sanitization utility found

Checklist Item: No unreachable code
Comments: No debugger statements or obviously dead code blocks found. 32 early-return guard clauses are expected patterns. 3 TODO comments in custom-image.js:62-72 mark unimplemented modal logic (stub code, not unreachable). ESLint airbnb-base includes no-unreachable rule which enforces this at lint time.
Evidence: No debugger statements found; 3 TODO stubs in blocks/custom-image/custom-image.js:62-72; ESLint no-unreachable enforced via airbnb-base

Checklist Item: No empty catch blocks
Comments: Two genuinely empty catch blocks found: story-card.js:590 has a comment-only catch (silently swallows index unavailability), story-card.js:859 falls back silently on URL normalization failure. brightcove-video.js:675 uses .catch(()=>{}) silently. video.js:231 catch returns silently. These suppress errors with no logging or user feedback.
Evidence: story-card.js:590 — empty catch (comment only); brightcove-video.js:675 — .catch(()=>{}); video.js:231 — catch { return; }

Checklist Item: WCAG 2.1 AA compliance met
Comments: Multiple WCAG 2.1 AA failures identified: (1) header.js:658 — invalid a>button nesting; (2) brightcove-video.js:944-948 — duplicate static IDs primaryVideoTitle/primaryVideoDesc when multiple instances on page; (3) eds-form.js:679-687 — error messages not linked via aria-describedby; (4) tag-utility-nav.js:99-117 — icon-only clear button with no aria-label (SC 4.1.2); (5) footer.js:34 and linklist.js:1252 — role=button on anchor without keyboard event parity; (6) story-card.css flex column-reverse causes visual/DOM tab order mismatch (SC 1.3.2, 2.4.3).
Evidence: header.js:658; brightcove-video.js:944-948; eds-form.js:679-687; tag-utility-nav.js:99-117; footer.js:34; linklist.js:1252; story-card.css:63,680,945

Checklist Item: Announce dynamic content changes
Comments: Only 5 blocks use aria-live or role=alert: tag-utility-nav, press-releases, find-an-injector, stock-ticker, search-input. Major dynamic content blocks lack announcements: carousel slide changes have no aria-live region; eds-form error messages (eds-form.js:679-687) have no role=alert; search results in search.js have no live region. Dynamic content changes in these blocks are invisible to screen reader users.
Evidence: carousel.js, eds-form.js, search.js — no aria-live or role=alert found; only 5 of ~25 dynamic blocks implement live regions

Checklist Item: Modal/dialog elements have role='dialog' or role='alertdialog' with aria-modal='true' and aria-labelledby/aria-label; keydown handler for Escape key present in source; focusable elements exist inside dialog container
Comments: Modal uses native HTML dialog element (showModal()) which provides role=dialog, focus trap, and Escape key handling natively — those are correct. However: (1) no aria-labelledby or aria-label on the dialog element itself (only close button has aria-label="Close"); (2) no aria-modal="true" (best practice for AT compatibility); (3) no explicit focus management to first focusable element after showModal(). Close button correctly has aria-label.
Evidence: blocks/modal/modal.js — uses dialog.showModal() (native a11y), but missing aria-labelledby on dialog element and aria-modal=true; no explicit post-open focus management

Checklist Item: <video> elements have <track kind='captions'> with valid non-empty src
Comments: Two blocks create native video elements: video.js:381 (self-hosted MP4 player) and hero-container.js:84 (background video). Neither adds a track element for captions. video.js creates the video element dynamically but never appends a track element even when captions are referenced in config. Brightcove uses video-js custom element (captions handled by Brightcove SDK separately).
Evidence: blocks/video/video.js:381-401 — native video element created without track element; blocks/hero-container/hero-container.js:84 — background video element, no track

Checklist Item: <audio> elements have adjacent transcript link or aria-describedby pointing to transcript content; transcript container visible (not display:none/visibility:hidden)
Comments: brightcove-podcast-player.js creates an audio-only Brightcove player but has no transcript link or aria-describedby pointing to transcript content. brightcove-video.js has extensive transcript config fields (transcriptType, transcriptLink, etc.) for video but the podcast player block has no transcript authoring fields or adjacent transcript link. Checklist Item: No focus-trap calls outside of modal/dialog components; no keydown handlers that programmatically restrict Tab key to a subset of elements on non-modal pages
Comments: No focus-trap library usage or custom Tab key interception found outside of the native dialog element (which handles focus trap correctly via showModal()). No keydown handlers that preventDefault on Tab key. No programmatic focus restriction on non-modal pages detected.

Comments: Multiple external scripts are dynamically injected without SRI: brightcove-video.js:234-235 loads Brightcove SDK from BC_PLAYERS_BASE CDN; maps.js:21-29 loads Google Maps API; recaptcha.js:18-32 loads reCAPTCHA; embed.js:9-12 loads Twitter embed scripts. Checklist Item: No hardcoded API keys or secrets in source

Checklist Item: No credentials or tokens in URLs or query parameters
Comments: No credentials, tokens, or secrets found embedded in URLs or query parameters. API keys are fetched at runtime from config endpoints and passed as variables, not constructed into URL strings with literal key values. Google Maps API key is appended via the loadGoogleMapsAPI function using the runtime value, not hardcoded in the URL.
Evidence: No key=/token=/secret= patterns found in fetch() calls or URL constructions in blocks/ or scripts

Checklist Item: No sensitive data in client-side comments or console logs
Comments: Console logs contain only error messages and diagnostic context (URLs, error objects) — no sensitive data, credentials, or PII logged. Checklist Item: Consistent error handling strategy
Comments: Error handling is inconsistent across blocks: some fetch calls check response.ok and throw (news-feed.js:17, eds-form.js:796, stock-ticker.js:185 — good pattern), others use if(resp.ok) without else handling the error case (form.js:63, quote.js:355, fact-card.js:289), others return [] silently on failure (pipeline-utility-nav.js:43). No centralized error handling utility — each block implements its own strategy independently. Empty catch blocks found in 3+ files (row 77). No global error boundary.

Comments: Only 12 console.error/warn calls exist for 61 catch blocks — meaning ~80% of catch blocks do not log errors. Errors that are logged are meaningful (network failures, form errors, URL validation). But the ratio of silent failures to logged errors is poor.

Checklist Item: Errors fail gracefully
Comments: Most blocks fail gracefully: find-an-injector.js has a dedicated showError() function with user-facing messaging; stock-ticker.js shows role=alert on error; story-card.js returns empty/null on parse errors allowing fallback rendering; search.js logs and continues; fragment.js returns null on missing fragment. Critical media blocks (brightcove-video, video) silently catch playback errors to avoid broken UI — acceptable for media autoplay. No uncaught exceptions that crash the page observed.
Evidence: find-an-injector.js:52-81 — dedicated showError() with user messaging; stock-ticker.js role=alert on error; story-card.js returns null/'' from catch blocks to enable fallback

Checklist Item: Avoid magic numbers
Comments: Magic numbers found across multiple files: maps.js:17, find-an-injector.js:439,572, recaptcha.js:14 all use 100ms setTimeout without named constants; quote.js:204 uses 200 as an undocumented string-length threshold; linklist.js:246 uses 10_000_000_000 as an inline epoch-detection threshold; editorial-feed.js:682 uses 300ms debounce inline; search-input.js:55 hardcodes maxLength=100. These should be named constants (POLLING_INTERVAL_MS, MAX_TEXT_LENGTH, DEBOUNCE_MS, etc.).
Evidence: maps.js:17 (100ms), find-an-injector.js:439,572 (100ms), recaptcha.js:14 (100ms), quote.js:204 (200 chars), linklist.js:246 (10_000_000_000), editorial-feed.js:682 (300ms) — all unnamed inline numbers

Checklist Item: Constants centralized
Comments: No centralized constants file exists. Each block defines its own module-level constants. The Brightcove CDN base URL is duplicated across 3 files (row 107). Magic numbers (100ms, 300ms, 200 chars) are scattered inline. scripts/utils.js and scripts/config.js exist but only provide utility functions — no shared constants module. A shared constants.js would prevent duplication of magic numbers and URLs.
Evidence: No scripts/constants.js or shared constants module; BC CDN URL duplicated across 3 files; magic timing/limit constants scattered inline across blocks

Checklist Item: Config-driven behavior
Comments: Config-driven behavior implemented via scripts/config.js with async getConfigValue() function. Used across blocks: story-card, quote, press-releases, find-an-injector, embed-form, stock-ticker, fact-card. API keys (gmapsKey, gtm-id) fetched at runtime via config, not hardcoded. scripts/delayed.js loads GTM ID via getConfigValue.
Evidence: getConfigValue used in scripts/config.js, scripts/delayed.js and 7 block files

Checklist Item: Shared utilities extracted
Comments: Shared utilities extracted into scripts/: utils.js (DOM helpers), index-utils.js (index fetching/filtering), config.js (runtime config), placeholders.js (i18n placeholders), cfUtil.js (content fragment utilities), multi-theme.js (theme switching), dompurify.min.js (sanitization). Good separation of shared concerns.
Evidence: scripts/utils.js, index-utils.js, config.js, placeholders.js, cfUtil.js, multi-theme.js

Checklist Item: Duplicate code percentage within standard threshold
Comments: Some duplicate logic patterns observed: pagination logic, sessionStorage caching patterns, and data-fetching/filtering repeated across linklist.js (1999 LOC), story-card.js (1159 LOC), and find-an-injector.js (871 LOC). linklist.js violates SRP with data fetching+filtering+sorting+rendering+pagination+tags in one file. No automated deduplication tooling (jscpd) found. Shared utilities exist (index-utils.js) but not fully utilized to reduce duplication in block files.
Evidence: linklist.js 1999 LOC (SRP violation); sessionStorage caching pattern repeated in 5+ blocks; no jscpd config

Checklist Item: No hardcoded environment values
Comments: No hardcoded localhost/staging/prod environment URLs found. Third-party base URLs are constants (BC_PLAYERS_BASE) or derived from config (gmapsKey via getConfigValue). External service URLs (brightcove.net, maps.googleapis.com) are fixed CDN endpoints, not environment-specific. API keys fetched via runtime config, not hardcoded.
Evidence: BC_PLAYERS_BASE constant in brightcove-video.js:6; gmapsKey via getConfigValue in maps.js; no localhost/staging URLs

Checklist Item: Avoid circular dependencies
Comments: No circular dependencies detected. Import graph: scripts.js → aem.js, utils.js; config.js → utils.js; index-utils.js → config.js, utils.js; cfUtil.js → config.js; delayed.js → config.js, multi-theme.js, aem.js; multi-theme.js → aem.js; editor-support.js → scripts.js, carousel.js, editor-support-rte.js. All dependencies are unidirectional with aem.js and utils.js as leaf nodes.
Evidence: Import graph analysis: scripts.js only imports aem.js/utils.js; no cycles found

Checklist Item: No stale TODO/FIXME comments; no comments referencing symbols that do not exist in scope; no commented-out code blocks
Comments: Stale TODOs and commented-out code found: (1) custom-image.js:62,65,72 — 3 TODO comments "Add logic for modal, to be covered as part of modal story development" (unresolved feature stubs); (2) scripts.js:282 — "TODO: add auto block, if needed" (stale placeholder); (3) carousel.js:467 — commented-out extractCarouselItemImage() function block (dead code). All three issues need cleanup.
Evidence: TODOs: custom-image.js:62,65,72; scripts.js:282; commented-out function: carousel.js:467

Checklist Item: commented-out code blocks >3 lines flagged; TODO/FIXME without issue-tracker reference flagged;
Comments: 4 TODO/FIXME without issue-tracker references: custom-image.js:62,65,72 and scripts.js:282 — none have JIRA/ticket references. Commented-out code block >3 lines: carousel.js:467-479 has 13+ commented-out lines (extractCarouselItemImage function). This dead code should be removed or tracked via version control.
Evidence: 4 TODOs without ticket refs; carousel.js:467-479 = 13-line commented-out function block

Checklist Item: Charset meta tag (<meta charset="UTF-8">) declared in <head> before any content-producing elements; charset declaration appears within first 1024 bytes of document
Comments: No <meta charset="UTF-8"> found in head.html, 404.html, or the rendered document head. EDS relies on the HTTP Content-Type response header (text/html; charset=utf-8) but the HTML-level charset declaration is missing. The meta charset tag should be the first element in <head> per HTML spec for browsers that detect encoding before HTTP headers.
Evidence: head.html: no charset meta; rendered page: charsetMeta=absent; HTTP header provides charset but HTML meta missing

Checklist Item: Language attribute (lang) on <html> element is a valid BCP 47 tag matching page content language; lang attribute not empty or set to 'undefined'
Comments: lang="en" set on <html> element — valid BCP 47 tag matching English page content. Not empty or undefined.
Evidence: document.documentElement.lang = "en"; validBCP47 = true

Checklist Item: Direction attribute (dir) specified on <html> or relevant container elements for RTL language pages; value is 'ltr' or 'rtl' matching content language direction
Comments: dir attribute not explicitly set on <html> element. Computed direction defaults to ltr (correct for English). Since this is English-only content, the omission is low risk, but dir="ltr" should be explicitly declared for completeness and RTL language readiness. No RTL language pages detected.
Evidence: document.documentElement.dir = (not set); computed direction = ltr

Checklist Item: If CSS print stylesheet (@media print) is defined, print styles hide non-essential UI elements (navigation/footer/sidebar/ads)
Comments: No @media print stylesheet defined anywhere in styles/ or blocks/. No print-specific CSS hiding navigation, footer, or non-essential UI. Medium/non-mandatory for a marketing site, but print styles would improve printability of content pages.
Evidence: grep @media print: 0 results across all CSS files

Checklist Item: Font-family declarations defined at :root or body level using CSS custom properties; component-level stylesheets reference font-family tokens instead of hardcoding font names
Comments: Font-family tokens defined in tokens.css :root (--body-font-family, --heading-font-family, etc.) — good. However, 2 blocks hardcode font names instead of using tokens: cta.css:322 hardcodes "Roboto Regular", Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif (should use var(--body-font-family)); editorial-feed.css:91 hardcodes 'Abbvie Icon' (should use var(--corp-icon-font-family)). tag-utility-nav uses font-family:inherit (acceptable).
Evidence: Hardcoded font-family: cta.css:322 ("Roboto Regular"), editorial-feed.css:91 ('Abbvie Icon')

Checklist Item: Content elements have explicit width and height HTML attributes or CSS aspect-ratio set to reserve layout space and prevent CLS
Comments: All 15 content images have explicit width/height attributes or CSS aspect-ratio set. Zero images missing dimension declarations. EDS picture element generation includes width/height attributes for layout space reservation.
Evidence: DOM check: 15 images, 0 missing width+height or aspect-ratio

Checklist Item: No unused npm/yarn packages in package.json; depcheck or npm-check analysis confirms all listed dependencies are imported in source code
Comments: All packages verified as used: gulp/postcss/postcss-import/postcss-custom-media/dotenv (gulpfile.js), eslint/airbnb-base/plugin-import/plugin-json/plugin-xwalk (@babel/eslint-parser in .eslintrc.js), husky (prepare hook), plop (scaffold:create), stylelint/stylelint-config-standard/stylelint-declaration-strict-value (CSS linting), merge-json-cli (build:json:models/definitions/filters scripts), chai (importer test suite). No depcheck tool available but manual verification shows all packages have defined uses.
Evidence: All devDeps have npm script or config references; chai used in tools/importer tests; merge-json-cli in build:json scripts

Checklist Item: No setState/dispatch/state-mutation calls inside for/while/do-while/forEach/map/reduce loops; state updates batched or moved outside iteration blocks
Comments: No state mutations (sessionStorage.setItem, localStorage.setItem, window.* assignments) or array mutations (.push/.splice) inside loop constructs found. State updates are performed outside iteration blocks.

Checklist Item: No unused component props passed from parent to child; unused prop detection via static analysis or TypeScript strict mode
Comments: EDS uses vanilla JS blocks — no component prop system (no React/Vue/Angular). Block decoration functions receive only the DOM block element. No parent-to-child prop passing pattern. TypeScript not used; ESLint with no-unused-vars enforced for standard variables. Not applicable in the traditional component-prop sense.
Evidence: EDS architecture: block functions receive DOM element, no prop system; ESLint no-unused-vars enabled

Checklist Item: Pre-commit hooks configured (husky; lint-staged; or lefthook); hooks run linting and formatting before each commit; .husky directory or equivalent present
